description: Fix CLI bugs discovered during backend audit: isPortInUse false positive on EACCES errors, and PID recycling risk in the stop command.

userStories.title: Fix isPortInUse false positive on non-EADDRINUSE errors
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want accurate port-in-use detection so that permission errors on privileged ports are reported correctly instead of showing a misleading 'port in use' message.
acceptanceCriteria: isPortInUse resolves true ONLY for EADDRINUSE errors
EACCES errors cause the start command to print a specific permission-denied error message mentioning the port number
Other net.Server errors are propagated/rejected (not silently mapped to 'port in use')
The server.close() is called in the error handler to clean up the server object

userStories.title: Mitigate PID recycling risk in stop command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want `opentabs stop` to verify the target process is actually an OpenTabs server before sending SIGTERM, so that PID recycling cannot accidentally kill an unrelated process.
acceptanceCriteria: Before sending SIGTERM, the stop command attempts a health check GET request to http://127.0.0.1:<port>/health
If the health check succeeds and the response includes the x-opentabs-version header, SIGTERM is sent as before
If the health check fails (connection refused, timeout, no x-opentabs-version header), the PID file is treated as stale: deleted with a message indicating the server is not running
The health check has a short timeout (2-3 seconds) to avoid hanging the stop command
If the port is unknown (no way to determine it), the stop command falls back to the existing behavior (SIGTERM with a warning)
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/stop.ts lines 58-83.

The current code reads the PID from ~/.opentabs/server.pid and sends SIGTERM without verification. To verify:

1. Read the port from the config or use the default port (check platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts for how the port is determined — it uses DEFAULT_PORT from @opentabs-dev/shared, which can be overridden by OPENTABS_PORT env var or config).
2. Read the auth secret from ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json (same as loadSecret in mcp-server config.ts).
3. Attempt: fetch(`http://127.0.0.1:${port}/health`, { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${secret}` }, signal: AbortSignal.timeout(2000) })
4. Check: response.ok && response.headers.get('x-opentabs-version')
5. If verified: proceed with SIGTERM as before
6. If not verified: clean up stale PID file, print message

The port can be read from process.env['OPENTABS_PORT'] or from the config file, or default to DEFAULT_PORT (9515). Check how other CLI commands determine the port — look at platform/cli/src/commands/status.ts or platform/cli/src/notify-server.ts for patterns.

Using opus because this requires reading multiple files to understand port/secret resolution patterns and integrating a health check into the existing stop flow.
